title: hieracium laurinum subsp. amygdalinum
intro: Hieracium laurinum subsp. amygdalinum, also known as hawkweed, is a plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which may help to soothe skin irritations and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Hieracium laurinum subsp. amygdalinum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its use as a diuretic to support kidney function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
